headline: $1000 Invested In Palantir Technologies 5 Years Ago Would Be Worth This Much Today
summary: Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 31.18% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 43.68%. Currently, Palantir Technologies has a market capitalization of $312.33 billion. Buying $1000 In PLTR: If an investor had bought $1000 of PLTR stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $6,356.30 today based on a price of $130.29 for PLTR at the time of writing. headline: Canaccord Raises C3.ai Price Target as Tom Siebel Returns to the CEO Seat
summary: Shares of C3.ai (NYSE:AI) caught a modest vote of confidence from Canaccord Genuity on May 13, as the firm raised its price target to $8 from $7 while keeping a Hold rating. headline: 5 Revealing Analyst Questions From Palantir Technologies’s Q1 Earnings Call
summary: Palantir Technologies delivered a first quarter marked by accelerating demand for its operational AI platform, particularly in the United States. Management credited broad-based adoption of its AI-enabled solutions across both commercial and government segments as the primary driver of performance, with U.S. revenue growth exceeding 100% year-over-year for the first time since its public debut. headline: Sift Names Co-Founder Austin Spiegel as CEO and Opens San Francisco Office
summary: Sift, the data infrastructure platform for physical AI, today announced that co-founder Austin Spiegel has been named Chief Executive Officer. The company also announced the opening of a second office in downtown San Francisco and the addition of senior leaders from Meta, Anduril, Applied Intuition, Grafana Labs, SpaceX, and Palantir to its 80-person team. Co-founder Karthik Gollapudi will shift his focus to product vision and industry evangelism.

Co-founder Karthik Gollapudi will shift his focus to product vision and industry evangelism.

headline: High Growth Tech Stocks In The US For May 2026
summary: The United States market has experienced a 1.5% increase over the last week and a substantial 26% rise over the past year, with earnings projected to grow by 17% annually. In this thriving environment, identifying high growth tech stocks involves looking for companies that demonstrate robust innovation and adaptability to capitalize on these favorable conditions.
